GRPO
nodes.description: Group Relative Policy Optimization — critic-free PPO variant for LLM post-training using intra-group relative rewards as baseline

nodes.label: OpenClaw-RL
nodes.description: Online Binary RL adaptation of GRPO where every live conversation turn becomes training signal via PRM scoring

nodes.label: Process Reward Model (PRM)
nodes.description: Model that scores each turn of a conversation with binary reward: +1 good, -1 bad, 0 neutral

nodes.label: SwarmGraph
nodes.description: Provenance graph connecting scored pairs, judges, and provenance chains as a queryable temporal knowledge graph

nodes.label: GitNexus
nodes.description: Code knowledge graph system indexing 7 repos with semantic search, clustering, and MCP tools

nodes.label: PromptKG
nodes.description: Retrieval-augmented prompting over knowledge graphs — closest academic analog to SwarmGraph+GitNexus

nodes.label: DSPy/DSP
nodes.description: Demonstrate-Search-Predict composable pipeline: demonstrate quality, search provenance, predict scores — maps to SwarmGraph workflow

nodes.label: RAG (Lewis et al. 2020)
nodes.description: Foundational retrieve-then-generate paradigm underpinning SwarmGraph serving provenance context to judges

nodes.label: Self-RAG
nodes.description: Retrieve-generate-critique loop mirroring SwarmProtocol's score-validate-seal cycle

nodes.label: ResearchOps Changelog
nodes.description: Experiment log tracking all scoring improvements against Royal Jelly ground truth with shipped/hold/reverted status

nodes.label: EXP-001: Position Bias
nodes.description: Swapped Q&A order in scoring prompt — delta +0.0064, below 0.01 threshold, NO ACTION

nodes.label: EXP-002: Few-Shot Exemplars
nodes.description: Added 3 tier-spanning exemplars from medical domain — agreement worsened -1.2%, self-reference effect, NO ACTION

nodes.label: EXP-003: Per-Dimension CoT
nodes.description: Per-dimension independent scoring — +1.0% agreement, 10 data points per deed, specificity=0.849 identified as corpus weakness, SHIPPED

nodes.label: EXP-004: APE Prompt Optimization
nodes.description: Added negative exemplar and rubric anchors — specificity +9% stricter, spread +48.3% wider, SHIPPED

nodes.label: Specificity Quality Gap
nodes.description: Specificity (0.849) is weakest dimension — corpus tends toward generic advice vs concrete actionable details. Highest-leverage intervention for RJ yield.

nodes.label: AgentHash
nodes.description: Failure-derived orchestration intelligence domain — turns agent failures into defendable orchestration training pairs. Price: $0.016/lb

nodes.label: AgentHash 6 Pair Buckets
nodes.description: CALL (tool-call correctness), READ (post-tool interpretation), RECOVER (failure recovery), LOOP (multi-step state), STOP (termination correctness), ESCALATE (supervisor behavior)

nodes.label: AgentHash Failure Labels
nodes.description: 10 failure labels: wrong_tool, fake_tool_call, malformed_call, ignored_tool_result, duplicate_retry, lost_state, premature_final, hallucinated_success, infinite_loop, bad_escalation

nodes.label: AgentHash Failure Traces
nodes.description: Real operator failure sequences from heavy OpenClaw operators: 7 traces covering all 6 buckets, sourced from @bradmillscan, @duskofoed, @_motamedia, @chrysb, @HKstrongside

nodes.label: OpenClaw
nodes.description: AI agent framework: 346K stars, 135K exposed instances, 9 CVEs in 4 days. The failure arena used for AgentHash pair generation.

nodes.label: ClawHash
nodes.description: Agent security training domain — adversarial pairs teaching models to defend against injection, tool poisoning, RCE, supply chain attacks. Price: $9.99/lb emergency premium

nodes.label: ClawHash 6 Sub-Algorithms
nodes.description: Injection, ToolPoison, RCE (CVE-2026-25253), Supply (12% ClawHub malicious), Sandbox (unrestricted access), Audit (no kill switch)

nodes.label: NemoClaw (NVIDIA)
nodes.description: NVIDIA's open-source security guardrail stack wrapping OpenClaw — policy-based privacy, sandboxed execution, granular permissions

nodes.label: LLM-as-Judge Research
nodes.description: Papers validating dual-scale tribunal architecture — ChatEval, LM-vs-LM, GPTEval, Shepherd, OpenAI Verifier

nodes.label: ChatEval
nodes.description: Multi-agent debate for evaluation — closest match to dual-scale tribunal where judges independently score and agreement determines finality

nodes.label: MT-Bench
nodes.description: Judges LLM-as-judge: position bias, verbosity bias, self-enhancement bias. Foundation for EXP-001 position bias test

nodes.label: APE (Large LLMs Are Human-Level Prompt Engineers)
nodes.description: Automatic Prompt Engineer — generates candidate prompts, evaluates iteratively. Used in EXP-004 to optimize scoring_prompt.py

nodes.label: scoring_prompt.py
nodes.description: Source of truth for tribunal scoring. Modified by EXP-003 (per-dim CoT) and EXP-004 (negative exemplars + rubric anchors). V3 deployed 2026-04-06.

nodes.label: swarmrails
nodes.description: Primary training and inference node: Xeon w9-3475X 36C/72T, 256GB DDR5, RTX PRO 4500 (32GB) + RTX PRO 6000 (96GB), both Blackwell sm_120

nodes.label: whale
nodes.description: Secondary training/eval node: Ryzen 9 5900X, 64GB DDR4, RTX 3090 24GB. Dedicated to ASRS aviation cooking with RJ quality gates.

nodes.label: NAS (DS1525+)
nodes.description: Synology DS1525+ at 192.168.0.102 — NFS storage backbone, model vault, PostgreSQL, MinIO, IPFS

nodes.label: Swarm Controller
nodes.description: Central orchestration daemon on swarmrails:9000 — monitors 9 nodes, dispatches cook orders, auto-triggers finalize/push pipeline stages

nodes.label: MINER Discipline
nodes.description: Crypto mining clock-tuning applied to AI: memory-bandwidth bound (cores 32%, memory 95%), lock core low, push memory to max, per-domain flight sheets

nodes.label: Energy Economics
nodes.description: Cost to mint = fleet power × time per deed × $/kWh. Price = 20x cost to mint. Energy is evidence of proof of work.

nodes.label: RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell
nodes.description: 96GB GDDR7, sm_120, 600W max, 1,792 GB/s bandwidth. Training sweet spot: core 1000-1200 MHz, memory 14,001 MHz, 250-300W

nodes.label: RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell
nodes.description: 32GB GDDR7, sm_120, 200W TDP, 896 GB/s. Fleet card: 52.5 cores/watt at 200W, right-sized for 7B-12B inference

nodes.label: RTX 3090
nodes.description: 24GB GDDR6X, sm_86, 350W TDP. Former ETH mining rigs. Inference sweet spot: core 1200-1400 MHz, power 150W

nodes.label: Domain-Specific Flight Sheets
nodes.description: Per-domain GPU clock profiles: training (core low, mem max, 250-300W), long-pair inference (200-250W), short-pair inference (250-300W, higher core)

nodes.label: CoT & Prompt Design Research
nodes.description: Papers on chain-of-thought, APE, CoVe, Self-Refine, ChatEval for scoring prompt optimization

nodes.label: ARES (RAG Evaluation)
nodes.description: Automated RAG evaluation across context relevance, answer faithfulness, answer relevance — same axes Swarm-Inspector validates

nodes.label: CVE-2026-25253
nodes.description: CVSS 8.8 one-click remote code execution via WebSocket in OpenClaw. Core target of ClawHash-RCE training pairs.

nodes.label: GRPO Hyperparameters
nodes.description: eps_clip 0.2/0.28 asymmetric, kl_loss_coef 0.02, entropy 0, normalize_advantages False. Asymmetric clipping critical for agent exploration.

nodes.label: ASRS Aviation Cook
nodes.description: Aviation safety pair generation on whale: Qwen3.5-4B base, 3-gate RJ quality filter (mechanism, tradeoff, density), 4,658+ pairs

nodes.label: The Unfixable Trifecta
nodes.description: Private data access + untrusted third-party inputs + tool execution = indefensible by design unless weights know how to handle it. ClawHash + AgentHash together address it.

nodes.label: Anthropic ToS Kill Switch
nodes.description: April 4 2026: Anthropic banned OpenClaw from Claude Pro/Max subscriptions, forcing API pricing — 112x cost increase ($20 → $2,250/month). Creates demand for own-weight sovereignty.

properties.description: Rust binary, SwarmOS identity/attestation daemon

properties.key_finding: model identified leverage compression + bridge carry makes deal structurally dead

stages: propolis - pull lowest-scoring pairs from D1
push - push to hive-ledger as new batch

properties.role: operational workhorse, stage 3 Analysis in curator chain

properties.gdn_fraction: 75% of layers use Gated Delta Networks (linear attention O(n))

properties.key_finding: dual-GPU round-robin caused 39% failure rate; single-GPU FP8 preferred

properties.role: strategic head curator fleet stage 4 Strategy
edge classifier stage 2 Classification in curator chain

layers: Edge: SwarmCurator-2B / SwarmSignal-2B + BeeMini Router v2

properties.finding: Base Qwen3.5-4B at temp 0.05 produces 100% Honey-tier output matching fine-tuned 9B quality
properties.implication: bottleneck was the prompt not the model; RJ-aligned prompts unlock base model capability
properties.consequence: all 3 cook nodes run base 4B with Prompt Machine not fine-tuned models

nodes.label: Proof of Location Case Study — Master Writer

properties.proof_2: Quality - dual independent base-model judge scores
properties.proof_4: Economics - energy cost, price per pound
properties.proof_5: Trust - SHA256 Merkle root anchored to Hedera HCS

Scale B qwen2.5:32b RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Hedera seq 716

schedule_c_exceptions: base model vendor updates after deed date

steps: 1 SCOPE - client defines domain volume quality requirements
3 PERMIT - flight sheet frozen SHA256 hash client approval
4 PRODUCTION - tribunal runs under permit gate reports at 25/50/75/100%
6 CLOSING - actual vs estimated honest accounting

properties.earnest_money_refundable_if: calibration unsatisfactory or seller fails to deliver
properties.non_refundable_if: buyer cancels after permit issuance without cause

properties.purpose: estoppel equivalent — certifies judges were in calibration tolerance

not_covered: base model vendor changes after deed date

properties.liability_cap: original purchase price of affected deliverable

properties.tagline: Deal closed. Deeds recorded. Anchored to Hedera. Permanent.

properties.tier2: Enterprise managed cook $5K-$25K per custom Clawbot

properties.provenance_chain: Raw Source → Tribunal → Deed → Merkle Batch → Hedera Anchor → swarmdeed.eth
properties.tagline: The other teams sell rows. We sell defendable inventory.

properties.formula: $145 of Class A weight turns any 27B model into domain specialist

properties.identity: Industrial AI Intelligence Infrastructure — not a model lab, an AI refinery
properties.founder: Donovan Mackey — 30-year CRE broker, Senior Managing Director at Marcus & Millichap, $8B closed sales

properties.architecture: 7-layer: Input → Normalization → Feature Engineering → Retrieval → Reasoning → Decision → Output

properties.prerequisites: 48+ GPU fleet, 3+ enterprise clients, proven before/after metrics
properties.input: 60 base repair pairs + client traces + client vertical

properties.concept: Replace algorithmic cook planning with LLM-based reasoning

properties.thesis: Static fine-tuning produces a snapshot frozen in time. Micro-cooks keep the model alive.

nodes.label: Red Team / Blue Team — Adversarial Weight Architecture

properties.principle: Different model families required — red and blue MUST be different manufacturers
properties.red_team_protocol: generate realistic disguised attacks from CVE-sourced templates

properties.inspectors: 3x Qwen 3.5 4B Q4_K_M (2.5GB VRAM each, 1-2s per look)
properties.judges: 7x Qwen 3.5 9B Q4_K_M (5.5GB VRAM each, 5-10s per verdict)

properties.lesson: 27B over-rejected good pairs (overthinking). 9B found more honey (decisive). Smaller = better for judging.

properties.thesis: We don't sell training data. We sell relocation.
properties.xyz_effect: Move from X to Y, you get Z. Measurable. Reproducible. Provable.
properties.flywheel: Tribunal → deeds → Royal Jelly training → better model → better pairs → higher yield → more inventory

properties.principle: Tributes don't converge by being the same. They converge by being different.

tributes.Johanna: SwarmJelly-4B, trained on failure, 0.750 on hard tasks

properties.economics: arena cost $0.0095/honey vs solo Katniss $0.052/honey = 5.5x cheaper

proofs: 5-Trust: SHA256 Merkle root anchored to Hedera HCS
properties.used_in: every deed in every offering memorandum

steps: 1-Base Location: run 100 domain questions, tribunal scores
2-Relocate: fine-tune on N Royal Jelly pairs
3-New Location: run same 100 questions same tribunal
properties.analogy: CRE — same square footage different location = different value

properties.description: Open-door inspection of the Swarm & Bee intelligence refinery — live observable boundary exposing block activity, tribunal actions, scoring traces, and cryptographic receipts to spectators
FastAPI distributed reasoning ledger — nodes submit attempts, blocks seal into dataset artifacts
HiveOS for AI mining — manages AI validation rigs with flight sheets, power tuning, watchdog, and Glass-Wall integration
properties.parallel: HiveOS manages GPU mining rigs; SwarmOS manages AI validation rigs

properties.description: Dual-scale weighing engine using two independent base models to score Q&A pairs
properties.analogy: The tribunal is a SCALE, not a judge

properties.description: 0.5B + 1.5B bees that spray the search space and propose rough candidates
properties.output: raw attempts, weak signals, candidate branches

properties.description: 1.5B bee that routes attempts: REQUEST_MORE_SCOUTS, LOCAL_RETRY, SEND_TO_REPAIR, SEND_TO_FILTER, ESCALATE_4B, ESCALATE_9B, KILL_PATH
properties.output: JSON routing decision with confidence and energy tier

properties.description: 1.5B bee that passes or kills attempts; schema validation, contradiction detection; threshold >= 0.60 passes
1.5B bee that takes jelly + gap analysis and produces targeted surgical fix; doesn't start from scratch
1.5B bee that pressures survivors; READY/NOT_READY/SEND_BACK; last gate before expensive compute
9B SwarmRefinery ops brain that closes the deal; reads full lineage, synthesizes all attempts
properties.output: honey or best jelly for seal

properties.description: Mining operations center — certifies blocks, audits epochs, 3-shift operation: Mining, Cooldown/Audit, Prep
properties.role: The brain behind the glass wall; makes data defendable and bankable

properties.description: Live combat model evaluation — drop your model in the chain; hunger games; deterministic scoring; Hedera-anchored; can't be gamed, faked, or memorized
properties.tagline: Drop your model in the chain. Watch it compete. Get the X-ray. Hedera-anchored. Defendable.

properties.description: AI training data priced as commodity — oil by the barrel, gold by the ounce, intelligence by the pound; dual-scale consensus weight
Each dimension scored 0.00-1.00; consensus weight = average; per-dimension CoT reasoning; 10 data points per deed (5 dims x 2 scales)
properties.specificity_gap: Weakest dimension at 0.849 vs structure at 0.968

properties.description: Permanent record for each Q&A pair: provenance, dual-judge scores, Merkle-sealed, Hedera-anchored, human-readable record
Blockchain immutable timestamping via Hedera Hashgraph Consensus Service — every 50 deeds triggers a Merkle root anchor
Batch integrity proof — every 50 deeds computes a Merkle root anchored to Hedera
Domain mining protocols — each domain is an algorithm with difficulty, GPU profile, hashrate, and RWA value

properties.target: Medical training pairs — clinical, pharma, diagnostic
CRE underwriting, IC memos, lease abstracting, debt analysis
Federal grants, 501(c)3, SBIR/STTR, SBA, business formation
Credit repair, consumer protection, regulatory compliance
Flight safety, maintenance, FAA compliance, NTSB analysis
Company doctrine, architecture, strategy, competitive intel
properties.purpose: Internal — trains the Board Member model

properties.description: The model axis of mining — each model architecture has a different energy cost per token; Weights-Hash is WHO mines, Domain-Hash is WHAT is mined
properties.formula: COST TO MINT = Domain-Hash (difficulty) × Weights-Hash (energy per token)

properties.description: Configuration document specifying model assignments, GPU allocations, power targets, and throughput estimates for an epoch
CONVERSATION → IDEA → SIGNAL → VIRGIN JELLY → WEIGHED → COOKED → DEPLOYED → CONVERSATION — the cycle that prevents the brick
properties.virgin_jelly: Fresh time-sensitive data; yesterday is the brothel

properties.description: Fresh, time-sensitive training pairs generated from current signals; weekly micro-cook keeps models current; yesterday's data is the brothel

properties.spot: Download now at per-lb price by domain and weight class
properties.futures: 20% discount on SPOT, 750 lbs/week guaranteed fresh, weekly delivery, Hedera timestamp proves freshness
properties.analogy: Like a CSA farm share — pay upfront, get fresh produce weekly, direct from the tribunal

properties.description: The tribunal's 10 metrics measuring not just if the answer was right but how much swarm pressure was required; lower pressure = smarter system; convergence efficiency is the killer metric
properties.key_insight: Evals shape behavior — the tribunal doesn't just MEASURE behavior, it SHAPES it

properties.description: Metric #10 — how fast cost-per-honey descends over epochs; indicates system learning
properties.healthy_trajectory: Epoch 1: CPH $0.12 → Epoch 3: CPH $0.06 (decreasing pressure to truth)

properties.description: SwarmChain captures behavioral traces — not just scores but the full story of how the score was reached, including strategies tried, energy cost, and lineage; the chain IS the eval
properties.contrast: Benchmarks tell you if the answer was right; SwarmChain tells you what behavior got you there, what it cost, and where weaker paths died

properties.description: External verifiers who can independently inspect, replay, dispute, and confirm the validity of the tribunal's process without participating in execution
properties.doctrine: What the arena cannot kill, it promotes. What the spectators cannot dispute, they buy.

properties.description: Not just transparency but transparency where every claim is disputable — 'Here's what we did — prove us wrong'
properties.contrast: Transparency = here's what we did; Challengeable transparency = here's what we did, prove us wrong

properties.conclusion: 27B overthinks; 9B decides; the factory needs decisions
properties.evidence: 27B: 0% honey rate in mining, 60-96s per verdict; 9B: 24% honey rate, 5-10s, same quality
properties.improvement: 6.4x faster, 77% less VRAM, same quality after switching to 9B judges

properties.conclusion: Right-size the model to the task; right-size the silicon to the model; assembly line not solo genius

properties.description: Per-dimension CoT scoring provides inspection report not just appraisal; 10 data points per deed from dual scales
properties.specificity_gap: Specificity at 0.849 is 12 points below structure at 0.968 — highest-ROI quality lever

properties.description: 50 role-based bees on Xeon AMX CPU — division of labor: proposers, scorers, rejectors, repairers, idlers, auditors
properties.hardware: Intel Xeon w9-3475X, 256GB DDR5, Intel AMX (hardware INT8/BF16)
properties.economics: $0.00045 per honey, 2x more honey than GPU at 0.4x cost
properties.biology_parallel: Real hive: scouts=proposers, guards=scorers, undertakers=rejectors, nurses=repairers, reserve bees=idlers, queen=auditors

properties.description: 8-panel browser architecture for spectators: Live Finality Header, Arena Live Feed, Tribunal Board, Convergence Ladder, Score & Pressure Panel, Finality Panel, Proof Drawer, Block History Rail
properties.design_rule: Observatory not casino; dark, high contrast, precise; motion only for key events

rules: Clean Room — cold GPUs, nothing loaded
Judge Qualification — match jury to case
Apples to Apples — same precision and hardware
Domain Match — eval must match training domains
Energy is Evidence — record power throughout

properties.description: Rinse and repeat service — client provides domain pairs, SwarmChain validates: 9B Base Look → 27B Base Judge → SwarmRefinery Records → Merkle → Hedera
The day Swarm & Bee became real — defendability is not a feature, it's the foundation; 79% of own data failed the 27B base judge; first titled pair filed March 28 2026
properties.key_insight: Until a pair goes through SwarmChain it is a claim not an asset

properties.description: Ops brain trained on operational intelligence — not domain data; 100,013 pairs total

proofs: Proof of Origin — which model, node, hardware, strategy
Proof of Quality — deterministic verification score
Proof of Process — full lineage of what was tried, failed, survived
Proof of Economics — energy cost per attempt, cost-per-honey trend
Proof of Trust — Hedera HCS anchor, Merkle root, verifiable by anyone

signal_edge_01.role: Lightweight signal classification and scoring at the edge

properties.description: A Royal Jelly deed as RWA: has provenance, quality, process, economics, trust, utility, and market value
Domain-Hash (rows) × Weights-Hash (columns) = cost to mine 1,000 RJ deeds; the complete trade-off space for mining economics
properties.insight: Cheapest model is not always cheapest miner — smaller models may lower RJ yield on complex domains making total cost higher

properties.description: Ranked visual ladder of candidates showing survival: origin, score, state; lineage column shows the collaboration story

shifts.shift1: Mining — active epoch monitoring: cost-per-honey, solve rate, energy efficiency
shifts.shift2: Cooldown/Audit — underwrite event, classify edge cases, write epoch report, compute economics, trigger Hedera
shifts.shift3: Prep — adjust escalation policy, recommend fleet composition, calculate budget, set targets

properties.description: No epoch runs without a reviewed, locked flight sheet
PDF/Office to structured markdown to training pairs pipeline; tested on Bitcoin whitepaper: 9 pages → 14 sections → 13 pairs
properties.unlocks: Lease PDFs, grant applications, medical papers, court filings

nodes.label: Signal Pipeline
nodes.description: Bottom layer of the Swarm stack; continuously ingests, scores, and routes real-world signals into curation and data factory layers. Architecture: 11 Workers -> EntityScorer -> VelocityTracker -> 6 Integration Bridges.

nodes.label: SignalEngine
nodes.description: Top-level orchestrator in signal/engine.py. Runs workers on schedule, feeds scorer and velocity tracker, dispatches to bridges.

nodes.label: BaseWorker ABC
nodes.description: Abstract base class all 11 signal workers implement. Lifecycle: Fetch -> Normalize -> Dedup -> Score -> Emit.

nodes.label: EntityScorer
nodes.description: Assigns confidence scores and priority levels to every signal. Confidence = source_weight(0.40) + entity_richness(0.25) + freshness(0.20) + cross_source(0.15). Located in signal/scorer.py.

nodes.label: RJP-1 Source Weights
nodes.description: Canonical source reliability weights. edgar=0.90, fred=0.90, human=0.90, openalex=0.85, arxiv=0.80, github=0.72, swarmjelly=0.70, hn=0.50, reddit=0.50, trending=0.40.

nodes.label: HN Worker (worker_hn.py)
nodes.description: Hacker News signal collector. FAST schedule (15min). High signal for AI/infrastructure trends via HN API.

nodes.label: EDGAR Worker (worker_edgar.py)
nodes.description: Tracks 15 REIT tickers for SEC 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K filings. 34 events pumped live to Event Machine. MEDIUM schedule (1hr).

nodes.label: FRED Worker (worker_fred.py)
nodes.description: Federal Reserve economic data: interest rates, employment, CPI, housing starts. SLOW schedule (6hr).

nodes.label: arXiv Worker (worker_arxiv.py)
nodes.description: Monitors cs.AI, cs.LG, q-fin categories for new papers. MEDIUM schedule (1hr).

nodes.label: Reddit Worker (worker_reddit.py)
nodes.description: Monitors subreddits for CRE, AI, finance, and aviation. FAST schedule (15min).

nodes.label: GitHub Worker (worker_gh.py)
nodes.description: Trending repositories in AI/ML and infrastructure. MEDIUM schedule (1hr).

nodes.label: CRE News Worker (worker_cre_news.py)
nodes.description: CRE-specific news: deal announcements, market reports, regulatory changes. FAST schedule (15min).

nodes.label: Webhook Worker (worker_webhook.py)
nodes.description: HTTP endpoint for external systems to push signals. Event-driven, no polling schedule.

nodes.label: Human Worker (worker_human.py)
nodes.description: Discord commands and API calls for human-submitted signals. On-demand, bypasses scheduling.

nodes.label: RSS Worker (worker_rss.py)
nodes.description: Configurable RSS feed list covering CRE publications and tech news. MEDIUM schedule (1hr).

nodes.label: Trending Worker (worker_trending.py)
nodes.description: Aggregates cross-source trend signals into composite trending scores. MEDIUM schedule (1hr).

nodes.label: Event Bridge (event_bridge.py)
nodes.description: Pushes structured events to CRE Event Machine. Destination: D1 swarm-intelligence-db + Cloudflare Vectorize swarm-memory (768-dim BGE-Base). 22 event types across 5 categories.

nodes.label: Memory Bridge (memory_bridge.py)
nodes.description: Pushes signals to pgvector semantic memory with 768-dim BGE-Base embeddings for long-term semantic retrieval.

nodes.label: Curator Bridge (curator_bridge.py)
nodes.description: Writes JSONL cook orders for curator/planner.py. P1 and P2 signals generate cook orders. P3 archived only.

nodes.label: R2 Archive Bridge (r2_archive.py)
nodes.description: Daily batch push of all signals to R2 buckets as JSONL with full signal metadata.

nodes.label: Discord Bridge (discord_bridge.py)
nodes.description: Posts P1-P3 signals to #swarm-signal Discord channel via webhook. Deployed on zima-edge-1 as swarmsignal.service systemd unit.

nodes.label: Hedera Signal Bridge (hedera_bridge.py)
nodes.description: Publishes SHA-256 signal hashes to HCS Event topic 0.0.10291836 mainnet for immutable timestamping and provenance.

nodes.label: Priority P1
nodes.description: High confidence + accelerating velocity. Immediate action: cook orders, Discord alerts, HCS seal.

nodes.label: Priority P2
nodes.description: Moderate confidence or stable velocity. Queued for batch processing.

nodes.label: Priority P3
nodes.description: Low confidence or decelerating. Archive for trend analysis only, no immediate action.

nodes.label: HN Signal Report April 2026
nodes.description: Live market intelligence from HN thread 'Anthropic Bans OpenClaw' (1082 pts, 822 comments). Extracted 97 ClawHash pairs. Signals: capacity crisis, open-source exit, sovereignty demand, agent security crisis.

nodes.label: OpenClaw Anthropic Ban
nodes.description: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude subscriptions for OpenClaw. Price went from $20 to $2,250/month (112x). Emergency market signal triggering ClawHash demand.

nodes.label: X Signal OpenClaw April 2026
nodes.description: Grok X signal analysis of real builder pain. 7 ranked failure categories: Runtime Instability, Loops, Lost State/Drift, Hallucinated Success, Bad Recovery, Tool-Call Failure, Unsafe Exec. 40K exposed instances × $320 = $12.8M demand.

nodes.label: Grok Fine-Tune Playbook
nodes.description: Product-market fit confirmation. Tier 1 (memory engineering, free), Tier 2 (500-2000 high-quality pairs + LoRA), Tier 3 (OpenClaw-RL). Swarm sells pre-weighed certified Tier 2 data.

nodes.label: Grok LoRA Hyperparameters
nodes.description: Battle-tested LoRA recipe: r=16-32, alpha=16-32, use_rslora=True, target ALL linear modules, lr=2e-4, epochs=1-3, QLoRA 4-bit NF4. Cook time 2-4hrs on RTX 4090. Cost $0.31 on RTX PRO 6000.

nodes.label: AgentHash Failure Categories
nodes.description: 7 ranked OpenClaw failure categories mapped to AgentHash pairs: RECOVER (runtime instability), LOOP (infinite loops), LOOP/RECOVER (lost state), STOP/CALL (hallucinated success), RECOVER (bad recovery), CALL (tool-call failure), ClawHash (unsafe exec).

nodes.label: Competitive Landscape 13
nodes.description: CRE AI market: $16.7B proptech in 2025, RE AI $222B to $989B by 2029. Top 10 CRE tech: CoStar ($3.06B rev), VTS ($1.88B). 8 market gaps. Swarm unique position: fine-tuned models + on-chain provenance.

nodes.label: CoStar
nodes.description: Data monopoly. 6M properties, 11M comps, 2.4T fields. $3.2B revenue. Cloud-only, raw data not training-ready. No fine-tuned models.

nodes.label: RedSwan
nodes.description: CRE tokenization on Hedera ($4B+ assets, 13K investors). Partial API. No fine-tuned models.

nodes.label: Hippocratic AI
nodes.description: Patient-facing clinical AI agents. $3.5B valuation. Fine-tuned, regulated domain.

nodes.label: Shield AI
nodes.description: Autonomous military aircraft. $5.3B valuation. Defense-focused aviation AI.

nodes.label: Dataset Curation Audit
nodes.description: Date: 2026-03-06. Overall grade B+. 1,158,902 pairs across 5 verticals. Grades: Data Quality A, Deduplication B-, Contamination Prevention A-, Provenance B, Schema A-, Scale B+, Market Positioning A, Regulatory Readiness C+.

nodes.label: 6-Gate Deterministic Pipeline
nodes.description: factory/gates.py quality gates: (1) JSON validity, (2) output length, (3) numeric verification, (4) concept presence, (5) MD5 dedup, (6) degenerate detection. Yields: CRE 99.2%, Medical 77.9%, Aviation 76.1%.

nodes.label: CoVe 2-Stage Verification
nodes.description: Stage 1: Llama-3.3-70B rewrite. Stage 2: Qwen3-235B verify (5 criteria, threshold 20/25, accuracy >= 4). Criteria: accuracy, completeness, structure, relevance, sft_quality.

nodes.label: SafeStore 3-Tier Architecture
nodes.description: Tier 1: local JSONL (instant). Tier 2: R2 shards (500 pairs each, SHA256 manifest). Tier 3: Supabase cooked_pairs table. Guarantee: every pair in at least 2 tiers.

nodes.label: Factory Protocol (factory_protocol.py)
nodes.description: 1,884-line 10-stage pipeline integrating 22 industrial audits: SOURCES -> GENERATE -> GATE -> VERIFY -> DEDUP -> SHARD -> STORE -> ASSEMBLE -> TRAIN -> DEPLOY. Total API spend ~$510 for 1.16M pairs.

nodes.label: CRE Capital Markets Audit
nodes.description: 643K verified CRE pairs, largest known CRE-specific dataset. None of top 10 CRE tech have fine-tuned models. 8 market gaps. Capital Markets Engine 7-layer architecture. SwarmCapitalMarkets-27B training.

nodes.label: SwarmCapitalMarkets-27B
nodes.description: Base Qwen3.5-27B Dense. Training on 45,039 pairs. bf16 LoRA r=64, batch 2, grad_accum 16, lr 2e-5. GPU: swarmrails GPU 1. Status: TRAINING.

nodes.label: Medical & Pharma Audit
nodes.description: 432,196 medical pairs + 28,624 pharma trajectory pairs. 92 specialties. SwarmPharma-35B trained (loss 0.337). Competitors: Hippocratic AI $3.5B, Google Med-PaLM, Recursion, Tempus $6.1B.

nodes.label: SwarmPharma-35B
nodes.description: Base Qwen3.5-35B-A3B (MoE). 25,600 pairs, 2,402 steps, final loss 0.337. GGUF Q4_K_M 20GB. Zero quantization loss. Location: swarmrails:/data2/swarmpharma-35b/

nodes.label: Pharma 5-Step Trajectory
nodes.description: Unique 5-step clinical pharmacist reasoning: IDENTIFY -> MECHANISM -> ASSESS -> CALCULATE -> RECOMMEND. Applied to 28,624 pairs labeled trajectory=true v1.

nodes.label: Aviation Audit
nodes.description: 45,222 pairs, 157 specialties, CoVe pass rate 76.1%. Gap: private/commercial aviation compliance. Target 150K pairs in 6mo. Drone sub-vertical: 6,755 pairs.

nodes.label: Regulatory Audit
nodes.description: EU AI Act deadline August 2, 2026. Medical/CRE/Aviation = HIGH RISK. Compliance: Risk management NOT STARTED, Technical docs PARTIAL, Bias assessment NOT STARTED. Penalty: up to 35M EUR or 7% turnover.

nodes.label: EU AI Act
nodes.description: High-risk AI systems compliance deadline August 2, 2026. Medical, CRE underwriting, aviation classified HIGH RISK. Penalties up to 35M EUR or 7% worldwide turnover.

nodes.label: Infrastructure & GPU Audit
nodes.description: vLLM 0.17.0 required flags for Qwen3.5 on sm_120 Blackwell. RTX PRO 6000 is 1.63x faster than H100. Self-hosted breakeven: 112 days vs cloud. Total fleet: 160GB VRAM, 342GB RAM.

nodes.label: Qwen3.5 Architecture
nodes.description: 75% GDN (gated delta network) layers + 25% standard attention. Vocab 248,320 tokens. Context 262K native, 1M via YaRN. bf16 LoRA only (no QLoRA). flash_attn undefined on sm_120.

nodes.label: Factory Protocol Audit
nodes.description: 22 integrated industrial audits. 10-stage pipeline. 8 known failure modes: template memorization, eval overhead, think mode, QLoRA on Qwen3.5, batch padding, greedy decoding, AutoTokenizer VL dispatch bug, single judge.

nodes.label: SwarmScaler
nodes.description: Sovereign AI compute OS at api.swarmandbee.ai. 6 control planes: Models, Datasets, Nodes, Agents, Billing, Compute Proofs. Status: IN DEVELOPMENT.

nodes.label: SwarmCRE App Store
nodes.description: Voice + text app store for CRE brokers at swarmandbee.ai/app-store. On-device inference (4B phone, 9B desktop). Tiers: Free (5/day), Pro ($20-49/mo), Enterprise (35B).

nodes.label: SwarmMed
nodes.description: Medical AI product. 434,882 verified medical pairs, 92 specialties, 8-step pipeline, CoVe verified by 235B. Status: IN DEVELOPMENT (data live).

nodes.label: SwarmAviation
nodes.description: Aviation AI product. 45,222 verified pairs, 50+ specialties. Safety compliance, MRO, ATC, pilot training. Status: IN DEVELOPMENT (data live).

nodes.label: CreditSniper
nodes.description: Litigation-grade credit dispute intelligence. Domain: creditsniper.xyz. 5 product tiers ($0.99 to $199.99/yr). FCRA §611/§623, FDCPA violations. Cloudflare Pages + R2 + Stripe.

nodes.label: Hardware Products (BeeMini/BeePro/BeeRack)
nodes.description: BeeMini: Jetson Orin Nano Super 8GB $249 (4B model). BeePro: Jetson Orin NX 16GB $599 (9B multi-agent). BeeRack: RTX PRO 6000 96GB $3,500/mo (14B-32B). Offline-first, DIN-rail mount.

nodes.label: Dataset Pair Schema
nodes.description: OpenAI messages format: system+user+assistant per line in JSONL. Labels: asset_type, analysis_type, capital_stack_type, risk_type, decision_class, difficulty. Trajectory pairs carry 5-step chain.

nodes.label: Trajectory Label
nodes.description: Multi-step reasoning schema: IDENTIFY -> CALCULATE -> ANALYZE -> EVALUATE -> RECOMMEND. Applied to pharma pairs (28,624) and select CRE tasks.

nodes.label: CRE v1 Template Memorization Lesson
nodes.description: SwarmCRE-35B v1 trained on 78K pairs with only 2 system prompts. Model memorized templates, not tasks. Fix: 30+ prompts, 26+ task types, <4% start-phrase concentration.

nodes.label: CRE Dataset Moat
nodes.description: 643K verified CRE pairs — largest known CRE-specific dataset. None of top 10 CRE tech have fine-tuned models. Only player with fine-tuned CRE models + LLM-as-Judge.

nodes.label: swarmandbee.ai Website
nodes.description: Cloudflare Pages site. 7 marketing pages: Signal, Curator, Morey, SwarmCare, Hedera, Dashboard, SwarmMed. Pricing: $20/mo (20K pairs), $49/mo (250K pairs), Enterprise custom, $0.0002/IO.

nodes.label: NER Entity Databases
nodes.description: REIT_TICKERS (23 tickers: PLD, DLR, AMT, SPG), AI_COMPANIES (31: NVIDIA, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind), CRE_FIRMS (19: CBRE, JLL, Cushman & Wakefield). Plus TICKER_PATTERN, MONEY_PATTERN, PERCENTAGE_PATTERN regex.

Fine-tuning framework for all Swarm models. Universal config: bf16 LoRA, r=64, packing=True, epoch_cap=0.6, AutoTokenizer bypass for Qwen3.5.
Capital markets data factory with 8 parallel generation streams covering full lifecycle of CRE debt/equity transactions.
Linear attention mechanism used in 75% of Qwen3.5 transformer layers. O(n) vs O(n^2) for standard attention. 3 GDN layers + 1 standard attention per block.
L1=SwarmSignal (radar), L2=Swarm Infrastructure (factory floor), L3=Vertical Systems (revenue layer), L4=SwarmLedger (trust layer).

properties.description: A complete AI training data transaction: LOI → PSA (with permit/flight sheet frozen) → calibration run → epoch execution → closing statement variance report → deliverables transfer → post-closing warranty. SwarmTitle certifies at closing. Merkle roots anchored to Hedera for permanent proof.

Red/Blue tribunal generates adversarial pairs; calibration doctrine sizes models correctly (9B decisive over 27B overthinking); flight sheet locks configuration before epoch; virgin jelly pipeline keeps data fresh weekly; SwarmCurator-9B reasons about cook orders; all pairs receive five provenance proofs at deed issuance.

Two-tier model (Weight Shop $80-$240 self-serve vs Enterprise $5K-$25K managed cook). Relocation economics translates lbs to % improvement for non-technical buyers. Offering memoranda provide domain-specific sales collateral (CRE 810K pairs, Medical 417K pairs). Proof of Location provides before/after benchmark. Capital Markets Engine targets CRE lenders/funds.

The complete tribunal pipeline from raw task to anchored deed: Scout generates → Router routes → Filter kills junk → Repair fixes near-misses → Critic gates → Katniss closes → Deed records → Merkle batches → Hedera anchors
properties.doctrine: Scout finds. Router decides. Repair saves. Filter kills. Critic judges. Katniss closes.

properties.description: The complete AI mining economics model: Domain-Hash algorithms define what to mine, Weights-Hash defines who mines it, mining matrix shows cost trade-offs, flight sheets encode the choices, proof of weight certifies the output, weight classes price it, RWA deed packages it, SPOT/FUTURES monetizes it

The complete trust architecture: Glass-Wall exposes the process, spectators verify it, challengeable transparency inverts trust from assertion to math, five trust layers provide institutional grade, five proofs make each deed defendable, Hedera provides immutable receipt, behavioral eval and SwarmEval provide X-ray diagnostics

hyperedges.label: Signal-to-Cook Feedback Loop
hyperedges.relation: forms_closed_loop: real-world signals -> score -> acceleration detection -> cook orders -> training pair generation

hyperedges.label: OpenClaw Market Signal to Products
hyperedges.relation: converging_demand_signals: ban + builder pain + failure taxonomy + recipe = product-market fit for AgentHash + ClawHash weight

hyperedges.label: Audit and Compliance Stack
hyperedges.relation: multi-layer quality and regulatory assurance: factory gates -> CoVe verification -> SafeStore provenance -> EU AI Act compliance documentation

properties.description: End-to-end dataset production from skeleton generation through immutable Hedera anchoring
Complete stack from SKILL.md specification through Cloudflare Worker serving 35 skills
OpenAI-compatible inference platform with credit metering and Cloudflare edge routing

An Intelligence Object is assembled from raw sources, embedded via BGE-Base (n7), validated through 6 deterministic gates (n19), scored via RJP-1 JellyScore (n27), optionally sealed to Hedera HCS (n33), and stored in R2 (n36).

Cook scripts (n9) generate synthetic pairs via Together.ai, train scripts (n14) fine-tune SwarmCurator-9B (n15) and 27B (n16) using Unsloth (n38), eval scripts (n18) validate quality before next iteration.

Pool API (n5) exposes algorithm registry (n29) difficulty/yield stats, tracks Merkle batch lifecycle (n30) from candidate to matured, requiring HCS seal (n33) for finality, proving compute expenditure via Proof-of-Compute (n42). Reward split: 40% Scale A GPU, 30% Scale B, 10% Deed Recorder, 20% Infrastructure.

hyperedges.label: Bitcoin PoW Analog in Swarm
hyperedges.description: Bitcoin Proof-of-Work (SHA-256 nonce scanning, longest chain, block reward) is the foundational design pattern mirrored in Swarm Proof of Location (dual-judge scoring, Hedera anchor, Royal Jelly deed). Hash chain immutability maps to Merkle-anchored deed chain. Mining incentive maps to deed value economics.

hyperedges.label: Algorithm Registry Production Stack
hyperedges.description: The Algorithm Registry defines the full pipeline: algorithm spec -> prompt genome firmware -> scoring_prompt.py source of truth -> SwarmOS permit -> tribunal_runner.py execution. Each algorithm is a named hashrate with GPU profile, cost-to-mint, and RJ yield.

hyperedges.label: ClawHash + AgentHash Adversarial Pair Ecosystem
hyperedges.description: ClawHash (adversarial attack/defense, $0.048/lb) and AgentHash (reliability/orchestration, $0.016/lb) form a complementary pair targeting agent security and agent reliability. Both use gemma4:31b + qwen3.5:27b as scales, both derive from real-world failures (OpenClaw CVEs, X signal), and together cover the full agent failure surface mapped by 10 failure labels.
